_comment_hooks: PostToolUse auto-runs `ruff format` on any .py edit (silently no-ops if file isn't .py or ruff isn't installed). PreToolUse blocks edits to backend/alembic/versions/*.py — applied migrations must be replaced by a new revision (`make revision msg=...`) to avoid DB/code divergence. Project-scoped; team-shared.
_comment_mcp: Pre-approves the two MCP servers defined in .mcp.json so every clone doesn't get prompted on first use. context7 = live docs for Next.js 16 / FastAPI / SQLAlchemy; postgres = direct queries against the dev DB.

hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.py) /Users/han/NYCU/cloud-native/Cloud-Native-Development-Project/backend/.venv/bin/ruff format "$f" ;; esac; } 2>/dev/null || true

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | { read -r f; case "$f" in */backend/alembic/versions/*.py) echo 'BLOCKED: Modifying applied Alembic migrations causes DB/code divergence. Generate a new revision with: make revision msg="..."' >&2; exit 2 ;; esac; }
